factors.summary: The company's debt leverage situation shows mixed signals. While the net debt to net capitalization ratio increased, indicating potential weakening of the balance sheet, there is no significant change in debt leverage as per the provided text.

evidence.text: Our net debt to net capitalization ratio increased to 51.5% at June 30, 2018 from 39.1% at December 31, 2017 . The increase in this ratio was driven primarily by the reduction in our net capitalization of $1.5 billion for the period due to the $906.8 million distribution of Apergy, $745.0 million in share repurchases including prepayment under an accelerated share repurchase program, and $142.3 million of dividends, offset by $271.4 million of current earnings.
No significant changes in debt leverage as per the provided text.

sentiment.label: neutral

factors.summary: Free cash flow decreased compared to the prior year period. The decrease was primarily due to lower cash flow from operations and higher capital expenditures. Additionally, higher investments in working capital and compensation payouts contributed to the decline, despite strong operating cash flows.

evidence.text: For the six months ended June 30, 2018 , we generated free cash flow of $78.4 million , representing 2.3% of revenue and 28.4% of earnings from continuing operations. Free cash flow for the six months ended June 30, 2018 decreased $40.9 million compared to the prior year period, primarily due to lower cash flow provided by operations, as previously noted, as well as higher capital expenditures.

Cash provided by operating activities decreased approximately $23.5 million compared to the prior year. ... Higher investments in working capital of $58.6 million relative to the prior year in support of growth in organic bookings.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Decrease in free cash flow due to lower cash flow from operations and higher capital expenditures.

factors.summary: The company is prioritizing shareholder returns through consistent dividend payments and active share repurchase programs, signaling confidence in its stock. evidence.text: The company is actively repurchasing shares, indicating confidence in its stock.

Dividends paid to shareholders totaled $142.3 million during the six months ended June 30, 2018. ... Board of Directors approved a new standing share repurchase authorization, whereby we may repurchase up to 20 million shares of our common stock through December 31, 2020.

On May 22, 2018, we entered into an accelerated share repurchase agreement... The ASR Program is scheduled to be completed in 2018, subject to postponement or acceleration under the terms of the ASR Agreement.

We have not drawn down any loans under the facility nor do we anticipate doing so. [...] We are not aware of any potential impairment to our liquidity and expect to remain in compliance with all of our debt covenants.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Consistent dividend payments and active share repurchase programs indicate confidence in the stock.

evidence.text: Adjusted working capital increased from December 31, 2017 by $128.3 million, or 13.1%, to $1.1 billion at June 30, 2018, which reflected an increase of $97.7 million in accounts receivable and an increase of $87.0 million in inventory.
No specific information provided on inventory levels or management of stock.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Item 2.  Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations
